original: Day *043: “Finally, I can finally do something with this place! Paragon’s Tech-thralls were easy to hijack. Unbeknownst to the zaibatsu, I’m building new airspace tech and genetic splicing methods. It’s hard keeping things secret, but Oriann’s been very helpful and loyal to me. What she lacks in height, she largely compensates with her ingenious intellect. Her tentacular arms are particularly handy, but she’s getting enamoured of her pet project. GU-1’s been disappointing: no matter how aggressive our tweaks are, she can’t execute any of her targets. Something must be wrong with the neuron-reconditioning process. Maybe it’s because her mind was still intact when she went through her surgical remodeling. We’ll wipe the GUs’ memories before modifying their bodies from now on.” Day *333: “Oriann left. She couldn’t bear my vision for Dhaal anymore. I know that she won’t betray me; she’s got nobody else but me on her side. She took over Paragon’s cybernetics engineering department after her predecessor met an unfortunate end at the hands of her own automated turrets. She’ll make a fine operative in their ranks. It’s unfortunate that she took GU-1 with her. That failed excuse for an XO-breaker belonged in the trash! What worth has [silly |a terminator |an executioner ] that spares her victims?” Day *383: “Aliens made first contact! I need to kick project E.G.G. into overdrive. With this new profit opportunity, the zaibatsu will cut my funds and destroy everything I created now that our tech is basically outmatched by offworlders — or is it?” “My work could push Dhaal 500 years in the future. The magma extractor, antigrav generators, fusion reactor, and automated factories are ready. I just need enough power to start the E.G.G’s thrusters. Two more days.”

The last entry doesn’t have a date, but its message is clear: “Fuck! The Zaibatsu found out about my plans! They’re coming! I released the T4L0Ns and locked all access to the most vital rooms. They cut me from the power grid, so the fusion reactor won’t have enough power to extract me from this disgusting mire. I won’t leave the E.G.G. alive — they won’t let me.” “I need a way out. Maybe... No! That’s too risky. On the other hand, staying in stasis here will just be another form of decay. AAAAARGH! I can’t believe I’m doing this! I’ll make them all pay a thousandfold for this! I’m placing my fate into your hands, Zero Plus. It’s a shot in the dark, but she’ll make it. She’s the only one who can protect my legacy now. After years of selfless work, I can finally take some time to rest.” The diary ends here. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ] was in a hurry. Her soft belly becomes rounderer by the second, reaching the size of an overripe watermelon as her womb is flooded with enough glowing blue seed to make a pregnant-looking nightlight of her! You ride the orgasmic tides together, moaning and shaking like mindless beasts. When your cum highs finally recede, you slowly rise from your pearl seat. The breathless zaika gasps weakly, drooling milk[pc.hasVagina | and [pc.girlCum] ]. A moment later, Paulette releases [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Zero Plus |Ovvi ]’s tail and slowly stands up, showering the poor nurse’s face with ample leftover juices. [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Paulette gently sits the former Peer - current condom - against the wall and cleans her a little. “Zero Plus? Can you hear me?” “O-One more minute, mommy,” the ivory white zaika sleepily replies. |Before the disgraced Peer can recover, your partner quickly ties her down with rubber cables and sits her against the wall. She checks on her maker for a good long while, making sure that the foaming, alabaster doctor’s still breathing before joining you. “Now we are even,” the former XO mumbles. Dabbebieth slowly floats next to you. “I’ll oversee her. I’ll leave the rest to you.” Right. You still have a ship to hijack and a mnemonic lock to find. Fortunately, you can do both from here. ]

original: With Ovvi out of the way, you can finally end this once and for all. You sit at her desk, connect your Codex to it, and link it to the oval remote. In the blink of an eye, the device blips, granting you absolute control over the Extraplanetary Guardian Galleon. Stars, it’s even more powerful than you first though! Half the weapons systems are inactive, and the ones that are online can easily threaten an U.G.C. flagship and its escort. “Alright. Let’s get out of here.” The entire superstructure resonates as the ship rises high over the Crucible. You retract the magma extractor and deactivate the weapon systems before opening the QCOMM link to the nearest Steele Tech contact. You wait a moment for someone to answer your surprise call. The screen flickers a couple of times before showing you a familiar face. “Darius! I’m glad to see you.” The cybernetic clerk sighs in relief. “The pleasure’s all mine. We tried to contact you for hours after the flying fortress lifted off! Where have you been?” Well... about that. “Don’t tell me you’re the one responsible for this chaos?” “I won’t tell you, then. It doesn’t make these seats less comfortable, though.” Darius sighs. “You always get yourself into trouble. Like father like [pc.mf |son |daughter ]. The Peers and the U.G.C. commanders in orbit freaked out when they saw this giant vessel soar through the sky and head to the volcanic plateau. They lost a couple scouting drones to its air defenses. Can you confirm that you are in control of the ship?” “We are, now,” you reply. “Wonderful. I’ll fill an extraordinary ownership claim for you and sort things out with the higher ups. Our legal and communication departments will be in for a rough night. Bring the ship to low orbit, our recovery teams will pick you up and secure it from here.” You reach for your Codex and send him pictures and files of the many things you encountered onboard. “We came across many mechanised leyaks and modded lifeforms — proceed with extreme caution. There should be two lifeforms near one of the hull accesses, another in the Psionic Enhancement Ward, two huge ones in the Investigative Viridiplantae Yard and Main Lab respectively, along with an unknown amount of roaming leyaks, and the four of us in the Restricted Lab.” “The four of you?” Paulette and Dabbebieth wave at Darius. “The more the merrier,” the former XO says. “And who’s the fourth one?” You frown. Before you can explain, Paulette reaches for the speaker. “Zero Plus, another victim of Dr. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]’s experiments. She helped us take control of her Mistress’s scientific vessel.” [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |The zaika in question bounces in front of the screen. “Heyo Mr. Robot man! How are you?” “Fine,” Darius casually answers. “Would you kindly move aside Ms. Plus? [pc.name] and I still have some things to settle.” Zero Plus giggles. “See you later, cutie!” |Behind you, Ovvi shudders. “T-Thanks Paulette. I don’t deserve it.” The former XO leaves the range of the screen and kneels in front of the tied Peer. “It’s because you don’t deserve it that you must earn it. No place in this galaxy will be safe, if you misbehave.” The defeated doctor wordlessly nods. Let’s hope she doesn’t start another mad project once your back is turned. ] “Our teams will extensively study and disarm this zaika galleon before its induction into the Steele Tech fleet. The scientific teams begged the financial department for a brand new science vessel — Dr. Lessau will be thrilled to have an early Xmas present. The recovery teams are inbound. They should arrive in two hours. If I were you, I’d find something to do in the meantime.” “See you on Dhaal, Darius.” “Safe travels [pc.name],” he replies before hanging down. You slowly turn the chair around to face Paulette and Dabbebieth. “We’ve got some time to unwind.” Paulette cocks an eyebrow. “Oh really? Well, I know exactly how to pass the time.”

original: [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Zero Plus gasps. “Watcha gonna do, Paullie Pally? We’re gonna play ga[silly |y ]mes?” “Not quite,” she mysteriously answers. |“What’s on your mind?” “Something neat,” she mysteriously answers. ] She leans over the desk and reaches for the keyboard. Her orange hand dances on the device in a blur of light. She scours the secret files, riding streams of raw, encrypted data as casually as she breathes unsteadily. After a minute of intense searching, you hear a hiss of pressurized air. A reinforced safe opens on the other side of the room. “Is it?” “Yes. The cabinet containing the GUs’ mnemonic locks. The key to my lost memories is here, a few steps away. I can almost feel it.” Dabbebieth floats between you. “Which one was it again? Case SR-369?” Paulette nods. “Indeed. I’ll finally know the truth about myself.” Without further ado, you step toward the huge safe. There’s hundreds of drawers. Each and everyone of them is filled by one of Ovvi’s invention: a Heisenberg-Doppler shield emitter here, a foldable energy scythe there, schematics for new engines, new power sources, new genetic therapies, new species — she studied and covered all the fields related to terraformation and space exploration. She didn’t lie about bringing zaika civilization to the next level! “MI- OP- Almost there,” Dabbebieth mutters, searching dozens of drawers at once with his lith tentacles. Next to you, Paulette slowly stares at the safe. You don’t know if she’s flabbergasted by Ovvi’s work or scared by the sheer amount of stolen data locked here, in Dr. [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]’s sanctum. In the blink of an eye, the former XO reaches for a small compartment near the bottom. The steel drawer seamlessly slides open, revealing a scanner-like device. A little tag identifies it as Mnemonic lockpick SR-369. GUs 1 to 17. Handle with care. “Finally. The key to my mind. We went through Hell and back to find it.” Indeed. Does she know how to use it? Behind you, [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Zero Plus pounces on her feet. “Me! Me! Me! I know how it works!” Paulette cocks an eyebrow. “Show me.” The bimbo nurse bounces next to her and takes the device from her hand. “You hold it against your head, scan your temple, then it zaps your brain where it’s all tangly!” |Ovvi grunts. “Hold it against your temple. It’ll untie the neuronal knot in your superego with a small jolt.” ] Your crewmate shrugs. “Ominous as expected. It doesn’t look strong enough to fry my brain or pierce my reinforced skull. It’s worth a try.” “Maybe sit down first,” Dabbebieth proposes. “You might be overwhelmed by the emotional feedback.” “That’s not a bad idea. You’ve become a lot more protective as of late,” she remarks, petting her friend’s helmeted head. The blue jellyfish blushes brightly. It’s heartwarming to see how far he’s grown from a scared leyak into a cosmic horror slayer. Without further ado, the former XO takes your place at the desk and plugs herself to the computer. Above, a projector sparks to life, beaming images from her systems on a bright holoscreen. “You deserve to see it, after all we went through,” Paulette comments before raising the mnemonic key to her temple. “It better not sting too much. I hate sudden jolts.” The device extends slowly, placing a little electrode on your crewmate’s scalp before deploying little antennas. They start flickering a moment later, filling the room with more orange lights. Paulette’s eyes go wide. They move left and right, up and down, unable to focus on anyone or anything. On the screen, dozens of images appear in quick succession. Most of them are mundane, featuring nothing but Lithium Flower rambling like an angsty teen to her faithful bodyguard. Stars, this girl had issues. Next to you, [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Zero Plus [silly |shares some popcorn with Dabbebieth, observing |and Dabbebieth attentively observe ] your friend’s revealed memories |Dabbebieth attentively observes your friend’s memories[silly | with a bag of popcorn. Wait, where did he get it? |. ] ] Suddenly, the display settles on a familiar scene. You recognize the setting: Ovvi’s Main Lab. The cruel Peer is nowhere to be seen, but you can clearly see Lithium Flower meddling with Paulette’s gear. “Alright GU-1, let’s see how you fare with this one.” A threat appears on past-Paulette’s visor. She swiftly turns around and raises her rocket-propelled fist at it. “[silly |Stop right there, you criminal scum! |Target acquired. Neutralizing! ]” she blares in a voice nothing like hers before lunging at the training robot. The machine suddenly reveals hardlight claws, buzzing saws, and laser weaponry that’d put the U.G.C’s special forces to shame. She nimbly evades the deadly beams and serrated blades, using her thrusters to perform daring dodges and mad swirls. In the blink of an eye, she punches the poor android’s arms off, leaving only its chest, legs, and head intact. “Nicely done GU-1. Now, terminate it.” “Compliance,” she answers, raising her fist at its face. She steps closer, revving her jet booster for a killing blow. Her visor flickers. Something’s not right. “C-Comply...ance.” Her arm stops its course at the last moment, missing the machine by a hair’s breadth. “Com- Comply- yance.” Lithium Flower stalks toward GU-1 on chromed tentacles. “Not again! The damn inhibitor’s on overdrive! You should be an obedient dolly! This droid killed your loved ones, all of them! It deserves no compassion, no mercy! What’s wrong with you?” Past Paulette doesn’t respond. You read no error on her display. Her body’s drugged and altered beyond acceptable limits and yet a spark of independent life remains in her. “I tried everything. Electroshocks, chemical overload, invasive surgery, but nothing seems to erase your subconscious entirely. You are a mystery GU-1. How did you survive [silly |the XO program cranked to 11? |what should have broken a regular XO tenfold? ]” Just then, Lithium Flower’s holophone rings. “Yes boss, whatsup?” “Not us, not yet at least. Did it work this time?” The bratty zaika frowns. “No. She spared it again. No matter the calibrations, she refuses to kill. For some reason, her body — as mutilated and altered as is — refuses to terminate another being, even an artificial one. I wonder why.” You hear Ovvi hum through the speaker. “Maybe it’s because of her mental state when she was indoctrinated. She wasn’t very stable when we got her.” Lithium Flower nods. “I remember perfectly how desperate she was. She needed a huge amount of money to protect her mother’s legacy. She was in tears through the entire interview. If she suffered loss just before her transformation, maybe it inadvertently conflicted with our treatment and subconsciously added a protection imperative to her body?” “If that’s the case, then there’s nothing we can do to fix her. GU-1 is a failure. Scrap her.” The small zaika gasps. “I-I can do it! We can remake her again — we have the technology.” Ovvi hisses. “You can keep her if you want. A merciful GU is no use to me. We’ll start GU-2’s indoctrination tomorrow, starting with the brainwashing this time. Don’t forget to remove this conversation from her logs before processing her. Ovvi over.” Lithium Flower sighs. “I’m sorry GU-1. You won’t be the [silly |Ultimate Lifeform |next step to zaika ev[silly |i |o ]lution ],” she says, sincerely disappointed. “Initiate protocol SR-369.” The projector turns down. Next to you, Paulette shakes her head. “I-It’s so clear to me now. I didn’t remember that she was the one that hooked me to their project. Stars, I hope Lappis got the money in the end.” Dabbebieth floats down to Paulette. “Since the Little Blossoms Shelter is still running, she certainly did. How are you feeling?” The amazonian cyborg shrugs. “I don’t know. On the one hand, I’m creeped out by all the stuff they hid from me, but on the other hand, I’m glad that they couldn’t erase me, no matter how hard they tried.” “Maybe your mother’s memories kept you alive, in spite of all the mutilations you went through.” “Yeah... Most of my logs are confessions and diaries from Lithium Flower. I... don’t feel like sharing this stuff. It feels like violating her privacy. Wait... the last two passages triggered after Dabbebieth’s capture and aboard the Gloaming!” The shy leyak raises a tentacle. “Here they are, the memories we were looking for! We’ll finally know what happened, back then.” Paulette breathes deeply. “Alright. Buckle up everyone, this one’s gonna be a rough ride.”

The screen turns back online, this time in an unknown but extremely fancy and futuristic place. “Lithium Flower’s meridian apartments,” Paulette murmurs. You hear a clamor coming from behind and metal clicking, as if you were being chased by a mechanical spider. Past Paulette’s visor recording turns to an infuriated Lithium Flower. The purple brat is fuming in front of an exquisite tank filled with rare sea creatures. You recognize some galactic species but a good number of them are completely unknown to your Codex. How many secrets did the Peer hide from the U.G.C? “I’ll make them pay for this! No one steals from me without facing the consequences!” she barks before turning to face Past Paulette. “Finally! GU-1, I command you to track Tjulmo and punish its captors — thoroughly. Use all your weapons to recover it.” “Roger,” the GU simply replies. She mechanically turns around and activates her sensors. Interestingly, the thieves left an ultraviolet signature on everything they touched. Someone’s got defective, leaking bionics! “Wait!” the shortstack zaika shouts from across the room. Past Paulette stops in her tracks. “Yes Mistress?” “Protocol RS-963. If your systems deem the rescue too dangerous, reject my initial orders and go back home. I don’t want to lose you too. Go — my flame — and give them hell!” The screen goes black. Well, that’s a surprise: Lithium Flower has a heart, albeit a very wicked one. She... cared for Paulette, or rather, the obedient, modded version of her. “I didn’t expect that,” your crewmate admits. “After all she’s done to me, all the pain, the humiliation, and forced alterations, I feel... no, I don’t feel. I should be angry, sad, vengeful, but I’m none of that — I’m just... mildly annoyed.” Dabbebieth taps her shoulder. “We beat her fair and square. Lithium Flower has no power over us anymore. The nightmare’s over.” What does Tjulmo mean? You heard Lithium Flower call Dabbebieth that name. “It’s old zaika for treasure, precious, extraordinary. She considered him very valuable,” Paulette replies, “but not enough to care about his health and rights as a living being.” The shy leyak sighs. “Like most leyak owners. Anyway, let’s check the last file. I wanna know how this story ends.”

original: The static disappears. Past Paulette’s mind is back on the Gloaming. Dozens of lasers target her helmet and chest, ready to annihilate her. She looks the little leyak in the eyes, basking into his dormant psyche. Error messages keep popping on her display, making it impossible for her to see anything. Without a warning, she forcibly rips the headpiece off from her sealed armor. Hundreds of microchips short-circuit and burn, creating a chain reaction into her mutilated brain. She drops her torn equipment to the ground before setting her eyes on the insectile captain. “Good. Now put the tank down an-” Past Paulette kicks her helmet, sending it flying at a cannon-bearing pirate. The improvised cannonball hits him square in the shoulder, making him pivot with the force of the impact. The shocked device triggers its explosive, final failsafe, spreading shrapnel and smoke all over the armory. The GU turns into a violet shadow, kicking, headbutting, striking, and tripping everything and everyone that comes in her way. Stray lasers and bullets bounce off her chestpiece, striping it away, one piece at a time. One by one, her foes collapse from the martial onslaught. Clad in the darkness, she’s unstoppable — almost. Suddenly, a crackling orb hits her at the back of her head, where her neckport connected to her helmet. Electromagnetic fields wreck havoc into her body, stunning her limbs and frying what little remained of her smart combat systems. Overwhelmed by pain and tearing forces, the defeated GU closes her eyes, forever. The screen goes dark. [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Dabbebieth, Zero Plus, and you |Dabbebeith and you ] stay still. Warm tears rain down Paulette’s cheeks. “I... had forgotten this conversation. It was the last time we talked — she passed a week later from a critical heart failure. Her last words — they echoed back to me. S-She saved me. M-Mom.” With that, the former XO burst into tears. Dabbebieth gently pats her shoulder, leaving her enough breathing room to deal with her unleashed emotions. She had this all inside her, locked away for so long, taken away by toxic chemicals, cruel machines, and crueler people. You went through all this, faced death again and again to get here, not knowing what would await you inside... But here you are, with your friend’s memories restored after thwarting the architect of evil’s plans. “Dabbebieth, [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Zero Plus, [pc.name] |[pc.name] ], thank you, for everything.” “It was a bumpy ride,” you simply reply. “I’m just glad that you’re alright.” The purple cyborg clears her misty eyes and smiles. “Yeah. I’d avoid the trip from the Main Lab to the Restricted Lab, if I had to do it again,” she jokingly sighs. You chill out a few more minutes before a new message prompts on your Codex. “Steele Tech recovery crew inbound. Ready for ext- [pc.name]? Is that you?” “M-Milly? Long time no see.” The dzaan pilot giggles. “Tell me about it. I heard that you needed a ride back home and I came here as [silly |fast as I could |soon as possible ].” “Hey! Don’t forget about us,” a rough voice barks behind her. “This is an official Steele Tech rescue mission, not a date.” “Here’s our coordinates. There’s also a zaika security guard, a gabilani, a vulpatra, a plant-modded zaika, a swarm of leyaks, and a [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |zaika nurse |zaika Peer ] on board with us. They’ll need a ride too, and protection from the zaibatsu.” “Acknowledged,” the rough voice replies. Milly recovers the QCOMM speaker. “We’ll dock to the [silly |sky |starport ] deck in a minute. See you soon, Steele.”

original: “Dhaal’s landing site is in view! We’ll arrive in about two minutes. Stars above, you know how to get yourself into trouble, [pc.name]. There was one, ONE zaika flying fortress and you managed to end up on board when it lifted off — I don’t know if I must be scared or impressed,” Milly says from the pilot’s seat. “By the way, we found the people you mentioned. Team C had to separate the gabilani hostage from the zaika bounty hunter. They were... extremely busy when they found them. As for the leyaks and the vulpatra bounty hunter, Team B fed emergency rations and led the former to safety before securing the latter. We came across the dryad and tauric zaika. It’ll be hard to find them a nice place to live and to keep them away from evil hands, but we’ll find a way.” “Good work.” The beta dzaan glances at [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Zero Plus |Ovvi ]. “What do we do with her?” Paulette steps in. “We’ll protect her from the zaibatsu. She was Dr. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]’s assistant, what little she remembers of her plans make her invaluable to them.” You nod in confirmation. “Gotcha,” the pilot answers. The shuttle’s door opens on Dhaal’s main spaceport. “Here you go! I hope you had a nice trip and appreciated the accommodations.” Paulette frowns. “[silly |Talk about low budget flights, no food or movies? I’m out of here! |The seats were comfortables but we could use more snacks. ]” Milly cocks an eyebrow. “You ate not one, but four bags of peanuts and your leyak drank all our soda.” “I was a little thirsty,” Dabbebieth apologizes, still wearing Paulette’s helmet. “Thanks for the ride, Milly. Take care of the E.G.G. for us.” The beta dzaan nods. “Will do.” Behind you, [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Zero Plus happily bounces off her seat and leaps on Paulette’s back. The former XO sighs, doing her best to stay calm with the squealing bimbo riding on her shoulders. She carries her and Dabbebieth down the ramp. You wave at the Steele Tech crew before joining the zaika. “See you around, [pc.name]. Don’t hesitate to call, if you need backup from the best recovery team of the galaxy,” Milly shouts, winking at you from inside the ship before the door closes. In the blink of an eye, the shuttle pierces the cloudy sky and soars toward the stars. You stare at it for long seconds before returning to the matter at hand: Zero Plus. “[pc.name]? Paullie Pally? Dabbidoo? Why are you making silly faces? Do I have something on my nose?” “No, it’s nothing like that. I was wondering what you wanted to do and where you wanted to go now that you are free from the E.G.G.” Zero Plus gawks at you. “I... dunno. Maybe I could come with you so we can have more fun together? Or... I could give huggies and kissies to hurt cuties? Gosh, thinking’s so hard!” |Ovvi weakly raises her head. She looks lost, tired. “What now?” Paulette places a hand on her shoulder. “[silly |Open your heart and you’ll see |Make the galaxy a better place, not just for zaika, but for everyone else ]. You’ve the body of a healer, so make use of it,” she simply says. “And if you misbehave, I’ll stalk the galaxy to correct you,” she adds. The shadow of a smile settles on Ovvi’s face. “I bet you would. Where are you taking me?” she asks Milly. “To a place where you can rebuild yourself away from the zaibatsu’s schemes.” Ovvi shrugs. “Sounds like prison.” “Don’t worry, it’s nothing like that. Our [silly |Minge |Meph-III ] outpost’s been looking for a competent nurse for months now. You won’t even need special goggles to see in the dark.” You quickly step back as the ramp raises and the turbines accelerate. Paulette stares at the rising shuttle for long seconds, following it as it pierces the clouds and reaches for the stars. When the little ship finally disappears, she finally relaxes. “So long, Dr. [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]. May you find redemption for your crimes.” You tap on her shoulder. “No need to be so dramatic.” Paulette slowly sets her eyes on you. “Correct,” she smiles, “I focused so much on her that I forgot about what truly matters,” she says, reaching for the mnemonic lock. Dabbebieth frowns. “Why did you keep it? It’s useless now.” “A little souvenir. I don’t wanna forget what happened this time. Thank you guys, for everything.” “What are you gonna do now?” you ask. “I’ll quickly visit my sisters. There’s something I need to share with them. Are you coming with lil’ guy?” Dabbebieth nods. Paulette places a peck on your right cheek. “I’ll be back soon. See you onboard, [pc.name].” With that, she deploys her wings and soars through the sky in the direction of the Little Blossoms Shelter. What an adventure! You could use a drink, or maybe two after that. There’s certainly a milk bar open nearby; there’s always one open on Dhaal. It’s good to be back in the Gyre, where only a few individuals actively want your demise. You’ll need a shower and lots of rest after all this action. Maybe Paulette will join you in bed and wind down together — she’s awfully good at snuggles for a mauve nugget. ]
context: <<POS:28606269>> textify(['\\n\\t<i>“Dhaal’s landing site is in view! We’ll arr'...])

original: “What do you like to do? If you want to settle down, doing something you like is primordial.” Zero Plus takes her chin in hand, adopting her super-thinking pose. “Hum... I like huggies, healing, and mech stuff. When I look at a cutie, my shiny glasses tell me everything I wanna know about them: breast size, girth, wetness, bounciness, and all the fancy techie stuff if they’ve got chromey flashy bitsies!” Dabbebieth hovers in front of her. “Maybe you could work as a physician, or as a cybernetician? The Gyre could use a zaika doctor who doesn’t charge an arm and a leg.” The albino bimbo gasps. “Yeah, I’d like that, but I don’t feel comfi- confa- safe in here. It’s all dull and creepy.” “Let me check something,” Paulette says, tapping on her glasses. The augmented accessory flashes with light. Metadata stolen from all around the Gyre quickly slides in front of her electric blue eyes. “Bingo!” “You found something Paullie Pally?” The former XO nods. “Indeed. There’s a clinic for cyborgs nearby. It’s owned by Sabrae, a former Paragon agent. I’m sure that she’ll be glad to have a bubbly nurse by her side.” “I dunno. What if I don’t like working with her or if the place is too scawwy?” Paulette smiles. “I’ll escort you there and do the talking for you. If you don’t want to stay, I’ll find you another place to live. Worst case scenario, there’s always the Little Blossoms Shelter.” The busty doll giggles. “What’s a Lil’ Bosoms Shelter?” “A wonderful place,” Paulette replies. “Come, I’ll show you around.” Without further ado, Zero Plus takes Paulette’s hand and holds her close. The former XO extends her wings, filling the air with bright green sparkles. Dabbebieth firmly grasps her shoulder as she starts flapping her wings and activates her rocket-fitted belt. What an adventure! You could use a drink, or maybe two after that. There’s certainly a milk bar open nearby; there’s always one open on Dhaal. It’s good to be back in the Gyre, where only a few individuals actively want your demise. You’ll need a shower and lots of rest after all this action. Maybe Paulette will join you in bed and wind down together — she’s awfully good at snuggles for a mauve nugget.

original: “There’s room on the [pc_ship.short]. We could keep her with us.” “Like, for realsies? Paullie Pally and I are gonna be roommates?” The former XO tilts her head. “I-If everyone’s fine with it, then sure.” “Will you handle it?” Dabbebieth asks. “In your shoes, I don’t know what I’d do or if I could stomach this at all.” Zero Plus gasps. “Paullie? You’ve got tummy aches? Do you need syrup?” Paulette sighs. “No, thank you. I’m fine,” she says, reaching for her hand. “Come on, let’s get you home.” “Home? I like the sound of that!” You take her other hand and guide her across the spaceport. She gasps in awe at the sight of the [pc_ship.short]. “Wooooah! It’s so big and shiny!” “And you haven’t seen how big it is on the inside,” you tease. The gushing bimbo gawks as you pass the bulkhead and guide her through the ship’s arteries. You can arrange and furnish it however you want.” Without a warning, the bimbo nurse jumps on the bed and bounces on it like she’s never had one before. “That’s sooooo cool! There’s something I need to share with them. Are you coming with lil’ guy?” Dabbebieth nods. Paulette places a wet peck on your right cheek. “I’ll be back soon. Don’t bounce on her bed too much while I’m away. I don’t want to fix a broken mattress,” she teases. She places another kiss on your cheek before heading toward the bulkhead for a quick trip to the Little Blossoms Shelter. Stars, that was an adventure! original: “We don’t need to neutralize her. We can just reset her,” you propose.
